page: Amazon Web Services m8i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. This x86_64 architecture system features 96 physical cores and 192 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The processor includes a substantial 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache, alongside hardware support for Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX).

Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 209,101.9 and a PassMark CPU score of 145,987.66. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, reaching 15,485.19 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 598.38 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The system also demonstrates strong capabilities in cryptographic operations and database tasks, registering 37,024.93 database operations on PassMark and 286.83 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. For machine learning workloads, the CPU-based LLM inference achieves 343.6 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model.

This bare-metal instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, meaning storage-heavy or GPU-dependent workloads must rely on external network-attached resources. However, it offers a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps to mitigate external storage latency. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-density compute resources, the instance is highly cost-efficient for large-scale enterprise applications, multi-threaded compilation, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-driven machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, supported by 768.0 GB of system memory. The hardware features advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX, alongside a massive 480 MB L3 cache. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high throughput in database operations and static web serving. Lacking local storage and dedicated GPUs, the instance relies on its 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external storage connectivity. It is highly suited for large-scale enterprise workloads, multi-threaded software compilation, high-throughput database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server featuring 192 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and 75 Gbps network bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and Intel Xeon processors for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and balanced memory for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors at 3.9 GHz
768.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-speed external connectivity
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
Best suited for enterprise applications, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference
